   129→
   130→
   131→# --- Socket.io events ---
   132→
   133→
   134→@sio.event
   135→async def connect(sid, environ):
   136→    print(f"[socket] Client connected: {sid}")
   137→
   138→
   139→@sio.event
   140→async def join_room(sid, data):
   141→    """Client joins a user-specific room for targeted events.
   142→
   143→    Supports both mirror_id (mirror display) and user_id (phone/Poke).
   144→    """
   145→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   146→    mirror_id = data.get("mirror_id")
   147→    room = mirror_id or user_id
   148→    if room:
   149→        await sio.enter_room(sid, room)
   150→        _sid_to_user[sid] = room
   151→        print(f"[socket] {sid} joined room {room}")
   152→
   153→
   154→@sio.event
   155→async def disconnect(sid):
   156→    print(f"[socket] Client disconnected: {sid}")
   157→    user_id = _sid_to_user.pop(sid, None)
   158→    if user_id and user_id in mira.sessions:
   159→        try:
   160→            await mira.end_session(user_id)
   161→            print(f"[socket] Cleaned up session for {user_id}")
   162→        except Exception as e:
   163→            print(f"[socket] Failed to clean up session for {user_id}: {e}")
   164→
   165→
   166→def _is_valid_uuid(value: str) -> bool:
   167→    try:
   168→        UUID(value)
   169→        return True
   170→    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
   171→        return False
   172→
   173→
   174→@sio.event
   175→async def start_session(sid, data):
   176→    """Start a Mira session for a user."""
   177→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   178→    if not user_id:
   179→        return
   180→
   181→    if not _is_valid_uuid(user_id):
   182→        print(f"[mira] Invalid UUID from {sid}: {user_id}")
   183→        await sio.emit(
   184→            "session_error",
   185→            {"error": "Invalid user ID: must be a valid UUID"},
   186→            to=sid,
   187→        )
   188→        return
   189→
   190→    print(f"[mira] Starting session for user {user_id}")
   191→    # Notify frontend that session is active (starts avatar + STT)
   192→    await sio.emit("session_active", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   193→    try:
   194→        await mira.start_session(user_id)
   195→    except Exception as e:
   196→        print(f"[mira] start_session failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   197→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Failed to start session: {e}"}, to=sid)
   198→
   199→
   200→@sio.event
   201→async def mirror_event(sid, data):
   202→    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
   203→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   204→    event = data.get("event", {})
   205→    if not user_id or not event:
   206→        return
   207→    event_type = event.get("type", "unknown")
   208→    if event_type == "voice":
   209→        print(f"[socket] mirror_event voice from {user_id}: {event.get('transcript', '')[:120]}")
   210→    else:
   211→        print(f"[socket] mirror_event {event_type} from {user_id}")
   212→    try:
   213→        await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)
   214→    except Exception as e:
   215→        print(f"[mira] mirror_event failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   216→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Event processing failed: {e}"}, to=sid)
   217→
   218→
   219→@sio.event
   220→async def photo_response(sid, data):
   221→    """Handle photo responses from the mirror (bypasses handle_event to avoid deadlock)."""
   222→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   223→    image_base64 = data.get("image_base64")
   224→    if not user_id or not image_base64:
   225→        return
   226→    resolved = mira.resolve_photo(user_id, image_base64)
   227→    if not resolved:
   228→        print(f"[socket] photo_response: no pending request for {user_id}")
   229→
   230→
   231→@sio.event
   232→async def end_session(sid, data):
   233→    """End a Mira session."""
   234→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   235→    if not user_id:
   236→        return
   237→    print(f"[mira] Ending session for user {user_id}")
   238→    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
   239→    if result:
   240→        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)
   241→
   242→
   243→@sio.event
   244→async def session_started(sid, data):
   245→    """
   246→    Auto-trigger recommendation generation when session starts via Socket.io.
   247→
   248→    Expected data: {
   249→        "session_id": "uuid",
   250→        "user_id": "uuid"
   251→    }
   252→    """
   253→    session_id = data.get("session_id")
   254→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   255→
   256→    if not session_id or not user_id:
   257→        await sio.emit("error", {"message": "Missing session_id or user_id"}, room=sid)
   258→        return
   259→
   260→    # Emit start event
   261→    await sio.emit("outfit_generation_started", {"session_id": session_id}, room=sid)
   262→
   263→    # Generate recommendations
   264→    db = await get_neon_client()
   265→    try:
   266→        result = await generate_outfit_recommendations(user_id, session_id, db)
   267→
   268→        # Emit as individual display_product payloads so the mirror's
   269→        # tool_result → ClothingCanvas path handles them (not outfits_ready)
   270→        if result.get("status") == "success" and result.get("data"):
   271→            payloads = _outfits_to_display_payloads(result["data"].get("outfits", []))
   272→            for payload in payloads:
   273→                await sio.emit("tool_result", payload, room=sid)
   274→
   275→    except Exception as e:
   276→        await sio.emit(
   277→            "error",
   278→            {"message": f"Failed to generate recommendations: {str(e)}"},
   279→            room=sid,
   280→        )
   281→
   282→    finally:
   283→        await db.close()
   284→
   285→
   286→# Wrap FastAPI with Socket.io — no outer CORS wrapper needed
   287→# (Socket.io has its own CORS via cors_allowed_origins, FastAPI has its own middleware)
   288→socket_app = socketio.ASGIApp(sio, other_asgi_app=app)
   289→

441→    async def _take_photo(self, user_id: str, reason: str) -> dict:
   442→        """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
   443→        if not self.sio:
   444→            return {"error": "Socket.io not available"}
   445→
   446→        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   447→        future: asyncio.Future[str] = loop.create_future()
   448→        self._pending_photos[user_id] = future
   449→
   450→        await self.sio.emit("request_photo", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   451→
   452→        try:
   453→            image_base64 = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   454→        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   455→            return {"error": "Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"}
   456→        finally:
   457→            self._pending_photos.pop(user_id, None)
   458→
   459→        if not image_base64:
   460→            return {"error": "Photo capture returned empty data"}
   461→
   462→        return {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
   463→
   464→    def resolve_photo(self, user_id: str, image_base64: str) -> bool:
   465→        """Resolve a pending photo Future. Returns True if resolved."""
   466→        future = self._pending_photos.get(user_id)
   467→        if future and not future.done():
   468→            future.set_result(image_base64)
   469→            return True
   470→        return False
   471→
   472→    async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   473→        """End a session and save summary."""
   474→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   475→        if not session:
   476→            return None
   477→
   478→        session.is_active = False
   479→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   480→
   481→        # Generate session summary via Claude
   482→        try:
   483→            summary = await self._generate_summary(session)
   484→        except Exception as e:
   485→            print(f"[mira] Failed to generate summary for {user_id}: {e}")
   486→            summary = "Session ended before summary could be generated."
   487→
   488→        # Save to DB — wrapped in error handling so a DB failure
   489→        # doesn't prevent the session_ended event from reaching the frontend
   490→        db = NeonHTTPClient()

### Current request_snapshot Handler (Lines 248–274)

The existing `request_snapshot` handler uses a **flat (non-nested) socket.emit format**:

```typescript
// Line 263-267
socket.emit("mirror_event", {
  type: "snapshot",
  image_base64: base64,

});
```

Here is the full report of all uncommitted (unstaged) changes:

**Summary:** 1 file changed, 2 insertions, 29 deletions.

**File:** `/Users/johnathanmo/mirrorless/frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`

There are two distinct changes in this file:

---

**Change 1: Restructured the `mirror_event` socket emission (lines ~264-267)**

The `socket.emit("mirror_event", ...)` payload was flattened. Before, the event data was nested under an `event` key. Now the fields are top-level:

```diff
       socket.emit("mirror_event", {
+        type: "snapshot",
+        image_base64: base64,
         user_id: userId,
-        event: { type: "snapshot", image_base64: base64 },
       });
```

- **Before:** `{ user_id, event: { type: "snapshot", image_base64: base64 } }`
- **After:** `{ type: "snapshot", image_base64: base64, user_id }`

---

**Change 2: Removed the on-demand photo request listener (28 lines deleted, lines ~275-302)**

The entire `useEffect` block that listened for `request_photo` socket events was removed. This block:

- Listened for `socket.on("request_photo", ...)` events

## Summary Report: Analysis of `take_photo` Tool in Tools.py

I've completed a thorough search of the backend agent tools and related files. Here are all my findings:

### PHOTO-RELATED DEFINITIONS IN tools.py

**Line 308-326: `take_photo` Tool Definition**

The `take_photo` tool is properly defined in `TOOL_DEFINITIONS`:
```
- Name: "take_photo"
- Description: "Take a photo of the user at the mirror right now..."
- Input schema: Requires "reason" parameter (string, required)
- Located: Lines 308-326 in tools.py
```

This tool is **legitimately defined** and not unusual.

### TOOL EXECUTION FLOW

**Line 439-440 in tools.py - execute_tool() handler:**
```python
elif tool_name == "take_photo":
    return {"error": "take_photo must be handled by the orchestrator"}
```

The `take_photo` tool is explicitly **routed away from execute_tool()** in tools.py because it requires special handling.

### ORCHESTRATOR HANDLING

**Lines 358-384 in orchestrator.py - _call_claude() method:**

The orchestrator intercepts `take_photo` calls:
1. **Line 359**: Checks if tool_use.name == "take_photo"
2. **Line 361-362**: Calls `self._take_photo()` with user_id and reason
3. **Line 367-382**: If successful, constructs a `tool_result_block` containing:
   - Text description: `"Photo captured ({reason}). Here is what the user looks like right now:"`
   - **Image data**: Base64-encoded JPEG as `{"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/jpeg", "data": photo_result["image_base64"]}}`
4. This image is **passed directly to Claude** for analysis (Claude receives the image)

**Lines 441-462 in orchestrator.py - _take_photo() method:**

```python
async def _take_photo(self, user_id: str, reason: str) -> dict:
    """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
    # Creates a future and stores it in _pending_photos[user_id]
    # Emits "request_photo" Socket.io event to the mirror
    # Waits for response with 5-second timeout (PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 43)
    # Returns: {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
```

**Lines 464-470 in orchestrator.py - resolve_photo() method:**

This resolves the pending photo future when the frontend responds.

### FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION GAP

**Mirror page (mirror/page.tsx) - CRITICAL FINDING:**

- **Line 270**: There IS a handler for `request_snapshot` events
- **MISSING**: There is **NO handler for `request_photo` events**

The frontend has:
```typescript
socket.on("request_snapshot", handleSnapshotRequest);  // Line 270
```

But there is **no corresponding `request_photo` handler**. This means:
1. If Claude calls `take_photo`, the orchestrator will emit `"request_photo"` (line 450 in orchestrator.py)
2. The frontend has **no listener** for this event
3. The orchestrator will **timeout after 5 seconds** (PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5)
4. Claude will receive an error: `"Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"`

### BACKEND PHOTO HANDLING

**Lines 220-228 in main.py - photo_response endpoint:**

```python
@sio.event
async def photo_response(sid, data):
    """Handle photo responses from the mirror (bypasses handle_event to avoid deadlock)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    image_base64 = data.get("image_base64")
    if not user_id or not image_base64:
        return
    resolved = mira.resolve_photo(user_id, image_base64)
```

This endpoint exists on the backend to receive photos, but the frontend never sends them because there's no `request_photo` listener.

### VERDICT

**No unusual or unauthorized additions found.** The `take_photo` tool is:
1. ✅ Properly defined in TOOL_DEFINITIONS (lines 308-326)
2. ✅ Properly handled in orchestrator.py
3. ✅ Has proper error handling and timeout logic
4. ✅ Returns image as base64 to Claude for analysis (legitimate use)

**However, there is a functional gap**: The frontend doesn't listen for `request_photo` events, so the tool cannot work. This appears to be **incomplete implementation** rather than malicious code.

```python
async def _take_photo(self, user_id: str, reason: str) -> dict:
    """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
    # Creates a future and stores it in _pending_photos[user_id]
    # Emits "request_photo" Socket.io event to the mirror
    # Waits for response with 5-second timeout (PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 43)
    # Returns: {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
```

**Lines 464-470 in orchestrator.py - resolve_photo() method:**

This resolves the pending photo future when the frontend responds.

### FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION GAP

**Mirror page (mirror/page.tsx) - CRITICAL FINDING:**

- **Line 270**: There IS a handler for `request_snapshot` events
- **MISSING**: There is **NO handler for `request_photo` events**

The frontend has:
```typescript
socket.on("request_snapshot", handleSnapshotRequest);  // Line 270
```

But there is **no corresponding `request_photo` handler**. This means:
1. If Claude calls `take_photo`, the orchestrator will emit `"request_photo"` (line 450 in orchestrator.py)
2. The frontend has **no listener** for this event
3. The orchestrator will **timeout after 5 seconds** (PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5)
4. Claude will receive an error: `"Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"`

### BACKEND PHOTO HANDLING

**Lines 220-228 in main.py - photo_response endpoint:**

```python
@sio.event
async def photo_response(sid, data):
    """Handle photo responses from the mirror (bypasses handle_event to avoid deadlock)."""
    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    image_base64 = data.get("image_base64")
    if not user_id or not image_base64:
        return
    resolved = mira.resolve_photo(user_id, image_base64)
```

This endpoint exists on the backend to receive photos, but the frontend never sends them because there's no `request_photo` listener.

### VERDICT

**No unusual or unauthorized additions found.** The `take_photo` tool is:
1. ✅ Properly defined in TOOL_DEFINITIONS (lines 308-326)
2. ✅ Properly handled in orchestrator.py
3. ✅ Has proper error handling and timeout logic
4. ✅ Returns image as base64 to Claude for analysis (legitimate use)

**However, there is a functional gap**: The frontend doesn't listen for `request_photo` events, so the tool cannot work. This appears to be **incomplete implementation** rather than malicious code.

+    async def _take_photo(self, user_id: str, reason: str) -> dict:
+        """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
+        if not self.sio:
+            return {"error": "Socket.io not available"}
+
+        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
+        future: asyncio.Future[str] = loop.create_future()
+        self._pending_photos[user_id] = future
+
+        await self.sio.emit("request_photo", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
+
+        try:
+            image_base64 = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
+        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
+            return {"error": "Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"}
+        finally:
+            self._pending_photos.pop(user_id, None)
+
+        if not image_base64:
+            return {"error": "Photo capture returned empty data"}
+
+        return {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
+
+    def resolve_photo(self, user_id: str, image_base64: str) -> bool:
+        """Resolve a pending photo Future. Returns True if resolved."""
+        future = self._pending_photos.get(user_id)
+        if future and not future.done():
+            future.set_result(image_base64)
+            return True
+        return False
+
     async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
         """End a session and save summary."""
         session = self.sessions.get(user_id)

+        """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
+        if not self.sio:
+            return {"error": "Socket.io not available"}
+
+        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
+        future: asyncio.Future[str] = loop.create_future()
+        self._pending_photos[user_id] = future
+
+        await self.sio.emit("request_photo", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
+
+        try:
+            image_base64 = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
+        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
+            return {"error": "Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"}
+        finally:
+            self._pending_photos.pop(user_id, None)
+
+        if not image_base64:
+            return {"error": "Photo capture returned empty data"}
+
+        return {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
+
+    def resolve_photo(self, user_id: str, image_base64: str) -> bool:
+        """Resolve a pending photo Future. Returns True if resolved."""
+        future = self._pending_photos.get(user_id)
+        if future and not future.done():
+            future.set_result(image_base64)
+            return True
+        return False
+
     async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
         """End a session and save summary."""
         session = self.sessions.get(user_id)

   180→
   181→```typescript
   182→useEffect(() => {
   183→  const handlePhotoRequest = () => {
   184→    const video = videoRef.current;
   185→    if (!video || video.readyState < video.HAVE_CURRENT_DATA) return;
   186→
   187→    const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
   188→    canvas.width = video.videoWidth;
   189→    canvas.height = video.videoHeight;
   190→    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
   191→    if (!ctx) return;
   192→
   193→    ctx.drawImage(video, 0, 0);
   194→    const dataUrl = canvas.toDataURL("image/jpeg", 0.7);
   195→    const base64 = dataUrl.split(",")[1];
   196→
   197→    socket.emit("photo_response", {
   198→      user_id: userId,
   199→      image_base64: base64,
   200→    });
   201→  };
   202→
   203→  socket.on("request_photo", handlePhotoRequest);
   204→  return () => { socket.off("request_photo", handlePhotoRequest); };

   208→**B. Fix existing `request_snapshot` handler** (line 263-267) — use correct nested format:

# Plan: Revert `take_photo` Tool (Committed in Orb Merge)

## Context

The `take_photo` tool was implemented and committed on the `orb` branch, which was then merged into `main` (commit `88ea175`). The frontend listener was reverted as an unstaged change, but all 3 backend files still contain the take_photo code **in committed state**. This half-reverted state breaks tool calls: Claude sees the `take_photo` tool definition, tries to call it, the orchestrator emits `request_photo` to the frontend, but no frontend listener exists — so it always times out after 5s and the error recovery bug in `_call_claude` corrupts conversation history permanently.

## What to Remove (3 backend files + 1 frontend file)

### 1. `backend/agent/tools.py`

**A. Remove `take_photo` tool definition** from `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` (lines 307-326 — the entire dict entry including trailing comma)

**B. Remove fallback dispatch** in `execute_tool()` (lines 439-440):
```python
elif tool_name == "take_photo":
    return {"error": "take_photo must be handled by the orchestrator"}
```

### 2. `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**A. Remove `PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5`** (line 43)

**B. Remove `self._pending_photos: dict[str, asyncio.Future] = {}`** from `__init__` (line 85)

**C. Remove `_select_model()` image routing** (lines 262-268 — the 7-line block that checks for image-containing tool results)

**D. Remove `take_photo` interception** in `_handle_tool_calls()` (lines 358-384 — the entire `if tool_use.name == "take_photo":` block with its 27 lines)

**E. Remove `_take_photo()` method** (lines 441-462)

**F. Remove `resolve_photo()` method** (lines 464-470)

### 3. `backend/main.py`

**Remove `photo_response` Socket.io handler** (lines 219-228 — the entire `@sio.event async def photo_response` function + blank line before it)

Also remove the `resolve_photo` import reference in line 10 if it exists (it's accessed as `mira.resolve_photo`, not imported directly, so likely nothing to do here).

### 4. `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`

**Already partially reverted** — the `request_photo` listener was already removed as an unstaged change. But the snapshot format was also changed from nested back to flat. We need to **keep the flat format revert** (this restores the original code) — so just leave `page.tsx` as-is (its current unstaged state is correct for a full revert).

## Verification

1. `pytest` from `backend/` — all existing tests should pass
2. Grep for `take_photo`, `_pending_photos`, `resolve_photo`, `request_photo`, `PHOTO_TIMEOUT` across the codebase — should return zero results
3. Start a session and verify normal tool calls (search_clothing, present_items, etc.) work without errors

-- **Session ends mid-photo**: Future times out, `finally` block cleans up
-- **No pending request**: `resolve_photo()` returns False, no crash
-- **Image size**: JPEG 0.7 quality ≈ 50-200KB base64, well within Anthropic's 20MB limit

-3. Test timeout: disconnect the mirror frontend, invoke take_photo, verify graceful 5s timeout
-4. Run `pytest` from `backend/` to check no regressions

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The root cause of the "still broken" state: the `orb` branch was merged into `main` before the local file reverts were committed. Git merges bring in the **committed** state from the source branch — any uncommitted working-tree reverts are silently overwritten. This is why `git checkout` showed "0 paths updated" — the working tree already matched the (broken) committed state.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Here's the summary: all 3 backend files need their take_photo code surgically removed via a new commit on `main`. The frontend `page.tsx` already has the correct reverts as unstaged changes. The plan details every line range to remove across the 4 files.

input.plan:
# Plan: Revert `take_photo` Tool (Committed in Orb Merge)

## Context

The `take_photo` tool was implemented and committed on the `orb` branch, which was then merged into `main` (commit `88ea175`). The frontend listener was reverted as an unstaged change, but all 3 backend files still contain the take_photo code **in committed state**. This half-reverted state breaks tool calls: Claude sees the `take_photo` tool definition, tries to call it, the orchestrator emits `request_photo` to the frontend, but no frontend listener exists — so it always times out after 5s and the error recovery bug in `_call_claude` corrupts conversation history permanently.

## What to Remove (3 backend files + 1 frontend file)

### 1. `backend/agent/tools.py`

**A. Remove `take_photo` tool definition** from `TOOL_DEFINITIONS` (lines 307-326 — the entire dict entry including trailing comma)

**B. Remove fallback dispatch** in `execute_tool()` (lines 439-440):
```python
elif tool_name == "take_photo":
    return {"error": "take_photo must be handled by the orchestrator"}
```

### 2. `backend/agent/orchestrator.py`

**A. Remove `PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5`** (line 43)

**B. Remove `self._pending_photos: dict[str, asyncio.Future] = {}`** from `__init__` (line 85)

**C. Remove `_select_model()` image routing** (lines 262-268 — the 7-line block that checks for image-containing tool results)

**D. Remove `take_photo` interception** in `_handle_tool_calls()` (lines 358-384 — the entire `if tool_use.name == "take_photo":` block with its 27 lines)

**E. Remove `_take_photo()` method** (lines 441-462)

**F. Remove `resolve_photo()` method** (lines 464-470)

### 3. `backend/main.py`

**Remove `photo_response` Socket.io handler** (lines 219-228 — the entire `@sio.event async def photo_response` function + blank line before it)

Also remove the `resolve_photo` import reference in line 10 if it exists (it's accessed as `mira.resolve_photo`, not imported directly, so likely nothing to do here).

### 4. `frontend/src/app/mirror/page.tsx`

**Already partially reverted** — the `request_photo` listener was already removed as an unstaged change. But the snapshot format was also changed from nested back to flat. We need to **keep the flat format revert** (this restores the original code) — so just leave `page.tsx` as-is (its current unstaged state is correct for a full revert).

## Verification

1. `pytest` from `backend/` — all existing tests should pass
2. Grep for `take_photo`, `_pending_photos`, `resolve_photo`, `request_photo`, `PHOTO_TIMEOUT` across the codebase — should return zero results
3. Start a session and verify normal tool calls (search_clothing, present_items, etc.) work without errors

1→"""Mira — Event-driven AI agent orchestrator.
     2→
     3→Receives events (voice, gestures, poses, silence) and orchestrates Claude API
     4→calls with tool use. Streams responses to HeyGen for voice output and broadcasts
     5→tool results to the frontend via Socket.io.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import json
    10→import os
    11→import time
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13→from typing import Dict, Optional
    14→from uuid import uuid4
    15→
    16→import anthropic
    17→from anthropic import AsyncAnthropic
    18→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    19→
    20→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt, build_recommendation_prompt, get_mira_system_prompt
    21→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    22→from agent.memory import (
    23→    load_user_profile,
    24→    load_user_purchases,
    25→    load_past_sessions,
    26→    load_purchase_statistics,
    27→    save_session_summary,
    28→    get_user_oauth_token,
    29→    refresh_calendar_events,
    30→)
    31→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    32→from services.user_data_service import (
    33→    get_user_profile_and_purchases,
    34→    is_new_user,
    35→    save_outfits_to_database,
    36→)
    37→
    38→load_dotenv()
    39→
    40→SONNET_MODEL = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"
    41→HAIKU_MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    42→SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
    43→PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
    44→EVENT_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 200
    45→SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20
    46→
    47→# Initialize Anthropic client for recommendation pipeline
    48→anthropic_client = AsyncAnthropic(api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))
    49→
    50→
    51→@dataclass
    52→class SessionState:
    53→    """Tracks state for a single mirror session."""
    54→
    55→    session_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()))
    56→    user_id: str = ""
    57→    api_calls: int = 0
    58→    items_shown: int = 0
    59→    likes: int = 0
    60→    dislikes: int = 0
    61→    liked_items: list = field(default_factory=list)
    62→    conversation_history: list = field(default_factory=list)
    63→    is_active: bool = True
    64→    last_input_time: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    65→    wrapping_up: bool = False
    66→    is_processing: bool = False
    67→    user_context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    68→    system_prompt: str = ""
    69→    _last_shown_item: dict | None = None
    70→    voice_messages: list = field(default_factory=list)
    71→
    72→
    73→class MiraOrchestrator:
    74→    """Event-driven orchestrator for the Mira AI stylist agent."""
    75→
    76→    def __init__(self, socket_io=None):
    77→        self.client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(
    78→            auth_token=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN"),
    79→            default_headers={"anthropic-beta": "oauth-2025-04-20"},
    80→            default_query={"beta": "true"},
    81→        )
    82→        self.sio = socket_io
    83→        self.sessions: dict[str, SessionState] = {}
    84→        self._silence_tasks: dict[str, asyncio.Task] = {}
    85→        self._pending_photos: dict[str, asyncio.Future] = {}
    86→
    87→    async def start_session(self, user_id: str) -> SessionState:
    88→        """Initialize a new Mira session for a user."""
    89→        # End any existing session to prevent orphaned timers
    90→        if user_id in self.sessions:
    91→            await self.end_session(user_id)
    92→
    93→        session = SessionState(user_id=user_id)
    94→        self.sessions[user_id] = session
    95→
    96→        # Load user data from DB in parallel — fall back to empty defaults if any fail
    97→        profile = {}
    98→        purchases = []
    99→        past_sessions = []
   100→        oauth_token = None
   101→        purchase_stats = {}
   102→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   103→        try:
   104→            profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token, purchase_stats = await asyncio.gather(
   105→                load_user_profile(db, user_id),
   106→                load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
   107→                load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
   108→                get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
   109→                load_purchase_statistics(db, user_id),
   110→            )
   111→        except Exception as e:
   112→            print(f"[mira] Failed to load user data for {user_id}, using defaults: {e}")
   113→        finally:
   114→            await db.close()
   115→
   116→        # Phase 2: Refresh calendar events from Google API (needs oauth_token from phase 1)
   117→        calendar_events = []
   118→        if oauth_token:
   119→            db = NeonHTTPClient()
   120→            try:
   121→                calendar_events = await refresh_calendar_events(db, user_id, oauth_token)
   122→                print(f"[mira] Loaded {len(calendar_events)} calendar events for {user_id}")
   123→            except Exception as e:
   124→                print(f"[mira] Calendar refresh failed for {user_id}, continuing without: {e}")
   125→            finally:
   126→                await db.close()
   127→
   128→        # Store on session for tool access
   129→        session.user_context = {
   130→            "profile": profile,
   131→            "purchases": purchases,
   132→            "past_sessions": past_sessions,
   133→            "oauth_token": oauth_token,
   134→            "purchase_stats": purchase_stats,
   135→            "calendar_events": calendar_events,
   136→        }
   137→
   138→        # Build system prompt with all user data
   139→        session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   140→            user_profile=profile,
   141→            purchases=purchases,
   142→            purchase_stats=purchase_stats,
   143→            calendar_events=calendar_events,
   144→            session_history=past_sessions,
   145→            session_state={
   146→                "items_shown": 0,
   147→                "likes": 0,
   148→                "dislikes": 0,
   149→                "api_calls": 0,
   150→            },
   151→        )
   152→
   153→        # Emit system prompt for debugging
   154→        if self.sio:
   155→            await self.sio.emit(
   156→                "debug_system_prompt",
   157→                {"prompt": session.system_prompt},
   158→                room=user_id,
   159→            )
   160→
   161→        # Create the DB session record (best-effort)
   162→        try:
   163→            db = NeonHTTPClient()
   164→            try:
   165→                await db.execute(
   166→                    "INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, started_at, status) "
   167→                    "VALUES ($1, $2::uuid, now(), 'active')",
   168→                    [session.session_id, user_id],
   169→                )
   170→            finally:
   171→                await db.close()
   172→        except Exception as e:
   173→            print(f"[mira] Failed to insert session record for {user_id}: {e}")
   174→
   175→        # Start silence detection
   176→        self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   177→
   178→        # Trigger Mira's opening line
   179→        await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   180→            "type": "session_start",
   181→            "message": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself and start the session.",
   182→        })
   183→
   184→        # Request a snapshot from the mirror so Mira can see the user
   185→        if self.sio:
   186→            await self.sio.emit("request_snapshot", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   187→
   188→        return session
   189→
   190→    async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
   191→        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.
   192→
   193→        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
   194→        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
   195→        """
   196→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   197→        if not session or not session.is_active:
   198→            print(f"[mira] handle_event: no active session for {user_id}, ignoring event {event.get('type', '?')}")
   199→            return
   200→
   201→        session.is_processing = True
   202→        try:
   203→            # Only restart the silence timer on real user input — not on
   204→            # silence events themselves, which would create a feedback loop
   205→            # (silence → Mira speaks → new timer → silence → repeat).
   206→            if event.get("type") != "silence":
   207→                session.last_input_time = time.time()
   208→                self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   209→
   210→            # Track gesture outcomes
   211→            gesture = event.get("gesture")
   212→
   213→            if gesture in ("thumbs_up", "swipe_right"):
   214→                session.likes += 1
   215→                if session._last_shown_item:
   216→                    session.liked_items.append(session._last_shown_item)
   217→            elif gesture in ("thumbs_down", "swipe_left"):
   218→                session.dislikes += 1
   219→
   220→            # Build user message from event
   221→            user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
   222→            session.conversation_history.append({
   223→                "role": "user",
   224→                "content": user_message,
   225→            })
   226→
   227→            # Update system prompt with current session state
   228→            session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   229→                user_profile=session.user_context.get("profile", {}),
   230→                purchases=session.user_context.get("purchases", []),
   231→                purchase_stats=session.user_context.get("purchase_stats"),
   232→                calendar_events=session.user_context.get("calendar_events"),
   233→                session_history=session.user_context.get("past_sessions", []),
   234→                session_state={
   235→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   236→                    "likes": session.likes,
   237→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   238→                    "api_calls": session.api_calls,
   239→                },
   240→            )
   241→
   242→            # Call Claude
   243→            try:
   244→                await self._call_claude(session)
   245→            except Exception as e:
   246→                print(f"[mira] Error calling Claude: {e}")
   247→                await self._stream_text(user_id, "Hmm, my brain glitched for a second. What were we talking about?")
   248→        finally:
   249→            session.is_processing = False
   250→
   251→    def _select_model(self, session: SessionState) -> tuple[str, int]:
   252→        """Select the right model and max_tokens for this turn.
   253→
   254→        Returns (model_id, max_tokens).
   255→        - Sonnet for conversational turns (speech quality matters)
   256→        - Haiku for tool-result processing (speed matters)
   257→        """
   258→        if session.conversation_history:
   259→            last_msg = session.conversation_history[-1]
   260→            # tool_result messages have list content with type: "tool_result" dicts
   261→            if last_msg.get("role") == "user" and isinstance(last_msg.get("content"), list):
   262→                # Use Sonnet for image-containing tool results (take_photo)
   263→                for block in last_msg["content"]:
   264→                    if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result":
   265→                        content = block.get("content")
   266→                        if isinstance(content, list) and any(
   267→                            isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("type") == "image" for c in content
   268→                        ):
   269→                            return SONNET_MODEL, 400
   270→                if any(
   271→                    isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result"
   272→                    for block in last_msg["content"]
   273→                ):
   274→                    return HAIKU_MODEL, 2048
   275→        return SONNET_MODEL, 400
   276→
   277→    async def _call_claude(self, session: SessionState, tool_depth: int = 0) -> None:
   278→        """Make a streaming Claude API call with tool use."""
   279→        if tool_depth >= 3:
   280→            print(f"[mira] Tool depth limit reached ({tool_depth}) for {session.user_id}, stopping")
   281→            return
   282→
   283→        if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
   284→            print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
   285→            return
   286→
   287→        session.api_calls += 1
   288→        model, max_tokens = self._select_model(session)
   289→        print(f"[mira] Using {model} (max_tokens={max_tokens}) for {session.user_id}")
   290→
   291→        # Collect full response (streaming to frontend happens via callback)
   292→        collected_text = ""
   293→        tool_uses = []
   294→        print(f"[mira] Calling Claude for {session.user_id} (turn #{session.api_calls})...")
   295→
   296→        try:
   297→            async with self.client.messages.stream(
   298→                model=model,
   299→                max_tokens=max_tokens,
   300→                system=session.system_prompt,
   301→                messages=session.conversation_history,
   302→                tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   303→            ) as stream:
   304→                async for event in stream:
   305→                    if event.type == "content_block_delta":
   306→                        if hasattr(event.delta, "text"):
   307→                            collected_text += event.delta.text
   308→                            # Stream each chunk to HeyGen / frontend
   309→                            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, event.delta.text)
   310→
   311→                    elif event.type == "content_block_stop":
   312→                        pass
   313→
   314→                # Get the final message for tool use blocks
   315→                final_message = await stream.get_final_message()
   316→
   317→            # Signal end-of-message so frontend flushes the sentence buffer
   318→            if collected_text:
   319→                print(f"[mira] AGENT SAID: {collected_text}")
   320→                await self._stream_text(session.user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   321→        except Exception as e:
   322→            print(f"[mira] Claude API call failed for {session.user_id}: {e}")
   323→            # Pop the last user message to keep conversation history consistent
   324→            if session.conversation_history and session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user":
   325→                session.conversation_history.pop()
   326→            # Emit a fallback message so the frontend user gets verbal feedback
   327→            fallback = "Hmm, my brain glitched for a second. What were we talking about?"
   328→            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, fallback)
   329→            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, "", end_of_message=True)
   330→            return
   331→
   332→        # Process any tool use blocks
   333→        for block in final_message.content:
   334→            if block.type == "tool_use":
   335→                tool_uses.append(block)
   336→
   337→        # Add assistant message to history
   338→        session.conversation_history.append({
   339→            "role": "assistant",
   340→            "content": final_message.content,
   341→        })
   342→
   343→        # Handle tool calls
   344→        if tool_uses:
   345→            await self._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses, tool_depth)
   346→
   347→    async def _handle_tool_calls(self, session: SessionState, tool_uses: list, tool_depth: int = 0) -> None:
   348→        """Execute tool calls and continue the conversation."""
   349→        tool_results = []
   350→
   351→        for tool_use in tool_uses:
   352→            # Log tool call with truncated input for terminal visibility
   353→            input_str = json.dumps(tool_use.input)
   354→            if len(input_str) > 200:
   355→                input_str = input_str[:200] + "..."
   356→            print(f"[mira] Tool call: {tool_use.name}({input_str})")
   357→
   358→            # take_photo is handled directly by the orchestrator (bypasses execute_tool)
   359→            if tool_use.name == "take_photo":
   360→                try:
   361→                    photo_result = await self._take_photo(
   362→                        session.user_id, tool_use.input.get("reason", "checking outfit")
   363→                    )
   364→                except Exception as e:
   365→                    photo_result = {"error": f"Photo capture failed: {e}"}
   366→
   367→                if "error" in photo_result:
   368→                    tool_result_block = {
   369→                        "type": "tool_result",
   370→                        "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   371→                        "content": json.dumps(photo_result),
   372→                        "is_error": True,
   373→                    }
   374→                else:
   375→                    tool_result_block = {
   376→                        "type": "tool_result",
   377→                        "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   378→                        "content": [
   379→                            {"type": "text", "text": f"Photo captured ({photo_result['reason']}). Here is what the user looks like right now:"},
   380→                            {"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/jpeg", "data": photo_result["image_base64"]}},
   381→                        ],
   382→                    }
   383→                tool_results.append(tool_result_block)
   384→                continue
   385→
   386→            try:
   387→                result = await execute_tool(
   388→                    tool_name=tool_use.name,
   389→                    tool_input=tool_use.input,
   390→                    user_context={
   391→                        "user_id": session.user_id,
   392→                        "session_id": session.session_id,
   393→                        "oauth_token": session.user_context.get("oauth_token"),
   394→                    },
   395→                )
   396→            except Exception as e:
   397→                print(f"[mira] Tool {tool_use.name} failed: {e}")
   398→                result = {"error": f"Tool execution failed: {str(e)}"}
   399→
   400→            # Parallel broadcast: send results to frontend via Socket.io
   401→            frontend_payload = result.pop("frontend_payload", None)
   402→            if frontend_payload and self.sio:
   403→                await self.sio.emit(
   404→                    "tool_result",
   405→                    frontend_payload,
   406→                    room=session.user_id,
   407→                )
   408→
   409→            # Cache voice messages for session history
   410→            if tool_use.name == "send_voice_to_client" and result.get("text"):
   411→                session.voice_messages.append({
   412→                    "text": result["text"],
   413→                    "emotion": result.get("emotion", "neutral"),
   414→                    "timestamp": time.time(),
   415→                })
   416→
   417→            # Track items shown (present_items + display_product count — search_clothing is invisible)
   418→            if tool_use.name in ("present_items", "display_product") and result.get("items"):
   419→                session.items_shown += len(result["items"])
   420→                if result["items"]:
   421→                    session._last_shown_item = result["items"][0]
   422→
   423→            tool_result_block = {
   424→                "type": "tool_result",
   425→                "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   426→                "content": json.dumps(result),
   427→            }
   428→            if "error" in result:
   429→                tool_result_block["is_error"] = True
   430→            tool_results.append(tool_result_block)
   431→
   432→        # Add tool results to conversation and continue
   433→        session.conversation_history.append({
   434→            "role": "user",
   435→            "content": tool_results,
   436→        })
   437→
   438→        # Call Claude again to process tool results
   439→        await self._call_claude(session, tool_depth=tool_depth + 1)
   440→
   441→    async def _take_photo(self, user_id: str, reason: str) -> dict:
   442→        """Request a photo from the mirror and wait for the response."""
   443→        if not self.sio:
   444→            return {"error": "Socket.io not available"}
   445→
   446→        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   447→        future: asyncio.Future[str] = loop.create_future()
   448→        self._pending_photos[user_id] = future
   449→
   450→        await self.sio.emit("request_photo", {"user_id": user_id}, room=user_id)
   451→
   452→        try:
   453→            image_base64 = await asyncio.wait_for(future, timeout=PHOTO_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   454→        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   455→            return {"error": "Photo capture timed out — the mirror camera may not be ready"}
   456→        finally:
   457→            self._pending_photos.pop(user_id, None)
   458→
   459→        if not image_base64:
   460→            return {"error": "Photo capture returned empty data"}
   461→
   462→        return {"success": True, "reason": reason, "image_base64": image_base64}
   463→
   464→    def resolve_photo(self, user_id: str, image_base64: str) -> bool:
   465→        """Resolve a pending photo Future. Returns True if resolved."""
   466→        future = self._pending_photos.get(user_id)
   467→        if future and not future.done():
   468→            future.set_result(image_base64)
   469→            return True
   470→        return False
   471→
   472→    async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   473→        """End a session and save summary."""
   474→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   475→        if not session:
   476→            return None
   477→
   478→        session.is_active = False
   479→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   480→
   481→        # Generate session summary via Claude
   482→        try:
   483→            summary = await self._generate_summary(session)
   484→        except Exception as e:
   485→            print(f"[mira] Failed to generate summary for {user_id}: {e}")
   486→            summary = "Session ended before summary could be generated."
   487→
   488→        # Save to DB — wrapped in error handling so a DB failure
   489→        # doesn't prevent the session_ended event from reaching the frontend
   490→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   491→        try:
   492→            await save_session_summary(
   493→                db=db,
   494→                session_id=session.session_id,
   495→                summary=summary,
   496→                liked_items=session.liked_items,
   497→                reactions={
   498→                    "likes": session.likes,
   499→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   500→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   501→                },
   502→            )
   503→        except Exception as e:
   504→            print(f"[mira] Failed to save session summary: {e}")
   505→        finally:
   506→            await db.close()
   507→
   508→        # Emit session end to frontend
   509→        if self.sio:
   510→            await self.sio.emit(
   511→                "session_ended",
   512→                {
   513→                    "session_id": session.session_id,
   514→                    "summary": summary,
   515→                    "liked_items": session.liked_items,
   516→                    "stats": {
   517→                        "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   518→                        "likes": session.likes,
   519→                        "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   520→                    },
   521→                },
   522→                room=user_id,
   523→            )
   524→
   525→        del self.sessions[user_id]
   526→        return {"summary": summary, "liked_items": session.liked_items}
   527→
   528→    async def _generate_summary(self, session: SessionState) -> str:
   529→        """Ask Claude to generate a short session summary for memory."""
   530→        response = await self.client.messages.create(
   531→            model=HAIKU_MODEL,
   532→            max_tokens=200,
   533→            system="Summarize this styling session in 2-3 sentences for future reference. Include key style preferences discovered, items liked, and overall vibe.",
   534→            messages=[
   535→                {
   536→                    "role": "user",
   537→                    "content": f"Session had {session.items_shown} items shown, {session.likes} liked, {session.dislikes} disliked. Liked items: {json.dumps(session.liked_items[:5])}",
   538→                }
   539→            ],
   540→        )
   541→        return response.content[0].text
   542→
   543→    def _event_to_message(self, event: dict) -> str | list:
   544→        """Convert an event dict to a Claude user message."""
   545→        event_type = event.get("type")
   546→
   547→        if event_type == "voice":
   548→            transcript = event.get("transcript", "")
   549→            print(f"[mira] USER SAID: {transcript}")
   550→            return transcript
   551→
   552→        elif event_type == "gesture":
   553→            gesture = event.get("gesture", "unknown")
   554→            gesture_descriptions = {
   555→                "swipe_right": "The user swiped right (like/next).",
   556→                "swipe_left": "The user swiped left (dislike/skip).",
   557→                "thumbs_up": "The user gave a thumbs up (like this item).",
   558→                "thumbs_down": "The user gave a thumbs down (dislike this item).",
   559→            }
   560→            return gesture_descriptions.get(gesture, f"The user made a {gesture} gesture.")
   561→
   562→        elif event_type == "pose":
   563→            return [
   564→                {"type": "text", "text": "The user struck a new pose. Here's what they look like:"},
   565→                {
   566→                    "type": "image",
   567→                    "source": {
   568→                        "type": "base64",
   569→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   570→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   571→                    },
   572→                },
   573→            ]
   574→
   575→        elif event_type == "snapshot":
   576→            return [
   577→                {"type": "text", "text": "Here's a snapshot of the user at the mirror:"},
   578→                {
   579→                    "type": "image",
   580→                    "source": {
   581→                        "type": "base64",
   582→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   583→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   584→                    },
   585→                },
   586→            ]
   587→
   588→        elif event_type == "silence":
   589→            duration = event.get("duration_seconds", SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   590→            return f"The user has been silent for {duration} seconds. Say something to keep the conversation going."
   591→
   592→        elif event_type == "session_start":
   593→            return event.get("message", "Start the session.")
   594→
   595→        return str(event)
   596→
   597→    async def _stream_text(self, user_id: str, text_chunk: str, end_of_message: bool = False) -> None:
   598→        """Stream a text chunk to the frontend.
   599→
   600→        When end_of_message is True, emits is_chunk=false so the frontend's
   601→        SentenceBuffer flushes any remaining text (the last sentence has no
   602→        trailing delimiter to trigger a boundary).
   603→        """
   604→        if end_of_message:
   605→            print(f"[mira] Stream end-of-message to {user_id}")
   606→        if self.sio:
   607→            await self.sio.emit(
   608→                "mira_speech",
   609→                {"text": text_chunk, "is_chunk": not end_of_message},
   610→                room=user_id,
   611→            )
   612→
   613→    def _start_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   614→        """Start or restart the silence detection timer."""
   615→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   616→
   617→        async def _silence_callback():
   618→            await asyncio.sleep(SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   619→            session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   620→            if session and session.is_active and not session.is_processing:
   621→                await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   622→                    "type": "silence",
   623→                    "duration_seconds": SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   624→                })
   625→
   626→        self._silence_tasks[user_id] = asyncio.create_task(_silence_callback())
   627→
   628→    def _cancel_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   629→        """Cancel the silence timer for a user."""
   630→        task = self._silence_tasks.pop(user_id, None)
   631→        if task and not task.done():
   632→            task.cancel()
   633→

   635→# --- Recommendation Pipeline (standalone functions for REST endpoints) ---
   636→
   637→
   638→def _outfits_to_display_payloads(outfits: list) -> list[dict]:
   639→    """Convert REST pipeline outfit format into display_product-style payloads.
   640→
   641→    Matches the frontend_payload shape emitted by the _display_product tool,
   642→    so the mirror's tool_result listener can handle both paths identically.
   643→    Items without flat lay images are skipped (raw product photos look wrong on overlay).
   644→    """
   645→    payloads = []
   646→    for outfit in outfits:
   647→        items = []
   648→        for oi in outfit.get("items", []):
   649→            item = oi.get("item", {})
   650→            if not item.get("cleaned_image_url") and not item.get("flat_image_url"):
   651→                continue
   652→            items.append({
   653→                "title": item.get("title", ""),
   654→                "price": item.get("price", ""),
   655→                "image_url": item.get("image_url", ""),
   656→                "product_id": item.get("product_id", ""),
   657→                "type": oi.get("type", ""),
   658→                "cleaned_image_url": item.get("cleaned_image_url"),
   659→                "flat_image_url": item.get("flat_image_url"),
   660→                "link": item.get("link", ""),
   661→                "source": item.get("source", ""),
   662→            })
   663→        if items:
   664→            payloads.append({
   665→                "type": "display_product",
   666→                "items": items,
   667→                "outfit_name": outfit.get("outfit_name", ""),
   668→            })
   669→    return payloads
   670→
   671→
   672→def _extract_json_from_text(text: str) -> Optional[dict]:
   673→    """Extract JSON from text that may contain markdown code blocks."""
   674→    try:
   675→        if "```json" in text:
   676→            json_start = text.find("```json") + 7
   677→            json_end = text.find("```", json_start)
   678→            return json.loads(text[json_start:json_end].strip())
   679→        elif "```" in text:
   680→            json_start = text.find("```") + 3
   681→            json_end = text.find("```", json_start)
   682→            return json.loads(text[json_start:json_end].strip())
   683→        else:
   684→            return json.loads(text.strip())
   685→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   686→        try:
   687→            start = text.find("{")
   688→            end = text.rfind("}") + 1
   689→            return json.loads(text[start:end])
   690→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
   691→            return None
   692→
   693→
   694→async def generate_outfit_recommendations(
   695→    user_id: str, session_id: str, db: NeonHTTPClient
   696→) -> Dict:
   697→    """
   698→    Orchestrate the full recommendation flow.
   699→
   700→    1. Fetch user data (profile + purchases + top brands)
   701→    2. Handle new users (check if needs onboarding)
   702→    3. Call Serper directly to fetch clothing items
   703→    4. Send user context + clothing items to Claude in a single call
   704→    5. Parse final JSON response
   705→    6. Generate flat lay images + save to DB in parallel
   706→    7. Return results
   707→    """
   708→    from agent.tools import execute_give_recommendation, _select_diverse_items
   709→    from services.serper_cache import serper_cache
   710→
   711→    start_time = time.time()
   712→
   713→    try:
   714→        # Step 1: Fetch user data
   715→        user_data = await get_user_profile_and_purchases(db, user_id)
   716→        if not user_data:
   717→            return create_error_response("user_not_found", "Unknown User")
   718→
   719→        # Step 2: Check if new user needs onboarding
   720→        if await is_new_user(db, user_id):
   721→            return {
   722→                "status": "needs_onboarding",
   723→                "message": "User needs to complete onboarding questionnaire",
   724→            }
   725→
   726→        # Step 3: Call Serper directly (no Claude tool loop)
   727→        top_brands = user_data.get("top_brands", [])
   728→        style_profile = user_data.get("style_profile")
   729→        gender = "mens"
   730→        if style_profile:
   731→            gender = style_profile.get("gender", "mens")
   732→
   733→        tool_input = {"brands": top_brands[:5] if top_brands else [], "gender": gender}
   734→        print(f"[Mira] Fetching clothing from Serper (brands={tool_input['brands']}, gender={gender})")
   735→        clothing_text = await execute_give_recommendation(tool_input, session_id)
   736→
   737→        if clothing_text.startswith("Error:") or clothing_text.startswith("No clothing"):
   738→            return create_error_response("no_results", user_data["user"]["name"])
   739→
   740→        # Step 4: Single Claude call with clothing items already in the prompt
   741→        cached_items = serper_cache.get(session_id) or []
   742→        tops = [i for i in cached_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "top"]
   743→        bottoms = [i for i in cached_items if i.get("clothing_category") == "bottom"]
   744→        limited_items = _select_diverse_items(tops, 10) + _select_diverse_items(bottoms, 10)
   745→
   746→        system_prompt = get_mira_system_prompt()
   747→        user_prompt = build_recommendation_prompt(user_data, limited_items)
   748→
   749→        # Step 4b: Pre-generate flat lays for ALL candidate items in parallel with Claude
   750→        async def _pregenerate_flat_lays():
   751→            """Generate flat lays for all candidate items while Claude thinks."""
   752→            try:
   753→                from services.gemini_flatlay import generate_flat_lays_batch
   754→                items_for_flatlay = [
   755→                    {"image_url": i["image_url"], "title": i["title"], "product_id": i["product_id"]}
   756→                    for i in limited_items if i.get("image_url") and i.get("product_id")
   757→                ]
   758→                if items_for_flatlay:
   759→                    print(f"[Mira] Generating flat lay images for {len(items_for_flatlay)} items...")
   760→                    return await generate_flat_lays_batch(items_for_flatlay)
   761→            except ImportError:
   762→                print("[Mira] Gemini flat lay service not available, skipping")
   763→            except Exception as e:
   764→                print(f"[Mira] Flat lay generation failed (non-fatal): {e}")
   765→            return {}
   766→
   767→        # Run Claude + flat lays in parallel
   768→        claude_response, flat_lay_map = await asyncio.gather(
   769→            anthropic_client.messages.create(
   770→                model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   771→                max_tokens=6144,
   772→                system=system_prompt,
   773→                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
   774→            ),
   775→            _pregenerate_flat_lays(),
   776→        )
   777→
   778→        # Extract JSON from Claude response
   779→        recommendations = None
   780→        for block in claude_response.content:
   781→            if hasattr(block, "text"):
   782→                recommendations = _extract_json_from_text(block.text)
   783→                break
   784→
   785→        if not recommendations:
   786→            return create_error_response("api_error", user_data["user"]["name"])
   787→
   788→        # Step 5: Map flat lays to selected outfit items + save to DB
   789→        outfits = recommendations.get("outfits", [])
   790→
   791→        # Attach flat lay images as both flat_image_url and cleaned_image_url
   792→        for outfit in outfits:
   793→            for outfit_item in outfit.get("items", []):
   794→                item = outfit_item.get("item", {})
   795→                pid = item.get("product_id", "")
   796→                if pid in flat_lay_map:
   797→                    item["flat_image_url"] = flat_lay_map[pid]
   798→                    item["cleaned_image_url"] = flat_lay_map[pid]
   799→
   800→        outfit_ids = await save_outfits_to_database(db, session_id, outfits)
   801→
   802→        # Attach database IDs to each outfit in the response
   803→        for outfit in outfits:
   804→            name = outfit.get("outfit_name", "")
   805→            if name in outfit_ids:
   806→                outfit["id"] = outfit_ids[name]
   807→
   808→        # Step 6: Return results
   809→        generation_time_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)
   810→
   811→        return {
   812→            "status": "success",
   813→            "data": recommendations,
   814→            "generation_time_ms": generation_time_ms,
   815→        }
   816→
   817→    except Exception as e:
   818→        print(f"Error generating recommendations: {e}")
   819→        return create_error_response("api_error", "there")
   820→
   821→
   822→def create_error_response(error_type: str, user_name: str) -> Dict:
   823→    """
   824→    Generate in-character error messages from Mira.
   825→
   826→    Types: "no_results", "new_user", "api_error", "no_brands", "user_not_found"
   827→    """
   828→    error_messages = {
   829→        "no_results": {
   830→            "status": "error",
   831→            "error_type": "no_results",
   832→            "message": f"Hey {user_name}! I tried searching for new pieces from your favorite brands, but I'm not finding much right now. This could be a temporary glitch with the shopping search. Want to try again in a minute?",
   833→        },
   834→        "new_user": {
   835→            "status": "needs_onboarding",
   836→            "message": f"Hi {user_name}! I'd love to help you pick out some outfits, but I don't know your style yet. Let's do a quick questionnaire so I can get to know your taste!",
   837→        },
   838→        "api_error": {
   839→            "status": "error",
   840→            "error_type": "api_error",
   841→            "message": f"Oof, {user_name} — something went wrong on my end. Technical difficulties! Give me a sec and let's try again.",
   842→        },
   843→        "no_brands": {
   844→            "status": "error",
   845→            "error_type": "no_brands",
   846→            "message": f"Hey {user_name}! I don't have any brands to search yet. Have you done any shopping recently, or want to tell me your favorite brands in the onboarding?",
   847→        },
   848→        "user_not_found": {
   849→            "status": "error",
   850→            "error_type": "user_not_found",
   851→            "message": "I can't find your profile. Are you logged in?",
   852→        },
   853→    }
   854→
   855→    return error_messages.get(error_type, error_messages["api_error"])
   856→
   857→
   858→async def update_outfit_reaction(
   859→    db: NeonHTTPClient, outfit_id: str, reaction: str
   860→) -> Dict:
   861→    """
   862→    Update user reaction for an outfit.
   863→
   864→    Args:
   865→        db: Database client
   866→        outfit_id: UUID of the outfit
   867→        reaction: "liked", "disliked", or "skipped"
   868→    """
   869→    try:
   870→        query = """
   871→            UPDATE session_outfits
   872→            SET reaction = $1
   873→            WHERE id = $2
   874→            RETURNING id
   875→        """
   876→        result = await db.execute(query, [reaction, outfit_id])
   877→
   878→        if not result:
   879→            return {"status": "error", "message": "Outfit not found"}
   880→
   881→        return {"status": "success"}
   882→
   883→    except Exception as e:
   884→        print(f"Error updating outfit reaction: {e}")
   885→        return {"status": "error", "message": "Failed to update reaction"}
   886→

1→"""Integration test for Mira agent — tests prompt building, tool execution, and a live Claude call."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import json
     5→import os
     6→import sys
     7→
     8→sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), ".."))
     9→
    10→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    11→
    12→load_dotenv()
    13→
    14→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt
    15→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    16→
    17→# Mock user data for testing
    18→MOCK_PROFILE = {
    19→    "user_id": "test-user-123",
    20→    "name": "Jordan",
    21→    "email": "jordan@test.com",
    22→    "brands": ["Nike", "Zara", "Uniqlo", "ASOS"],
    23→    "price_range": {"min": 20, "max": 150, "avg": 55},
    24→    "style_tags": ["streetwear", "minimalist", "casual"],
    25→    "narrative_summary": "Leans into streetwear basics with clean lines. Heavy Nike loyalty. Shops frequently at fast fashion retailers.",
    26→}
    27→
    28→MOCK_PURCHASES = [
    29→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Air Force 1 '07", "category": "shoes", "price": 115.0, "date": "2026-02-10"},
    30→    {"brand": "Zara", "item_name": "Oversized Blazer", "category": "outerwear", "price": 89.99, "date": "2026-02-05"},
    31→    {"brand": "Uniqlo", "item_name": "Heattech Crew Neck T-Shirt", "category": "tops", "price": 14.90, "date": "2026-01-28"},
    32→    {"brand": "ASOS", "item_name": "Slim Fit Chinos", "category": "bottoms", "price": 35.00, "date": "2026-01-10"},
    33→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Tech Fleece Joggers", "category": "bottoms", "price": 110.0, "date": "2025-12-15"},
    34→    {"brand": "Zara", "item_name": "Minimalist Leather Belt", "category": "accessories", "price": 29.90, "date": "2025-12-01"},
    35→    {"brand": "ASOS", "item_name": "Oversized Hoodie", "category": "tops", "price": 42.00, "date": "2025-11-28"},
    36→    {"brand": "Nike", "item_name": "Dunk Low Retro", "category": "shoes", "price": 110.0, "date": "2025-11-15"},
    37→]
    38→
    39→MOCK_PAST_SESSIONS = [
    40→    {"summary": "Jordan liked a Zara bomber jacket and Nike Blazers. Vibed with monochrome looks. Passed on anything too colorful."},
    41→]
    42→
    43→
    44→def test_system_prompt():
    45→    """Test that the system prompt builds correctly with user data."""
    46→    print("\n=== TEST 1: System Prompt Building ===\n")
    47→
    48→    prompt = build_system_prompt(
    49→        user_profile=MOCK_PROFILE,
    50→        purchases=MOCK_PURCHASES,
    51→        session_history=MOCK_PAST_SESSIONS,
    52→        session_state={"items_shown": 3, "likes": 1, "dislikes": 2, "api_calls": 5},
    53→    )
    54→
    55→    print(f"Prompt length: {len(prompt)} chars")
    56→    assert "Jordan" in prompt, "User name should be in prompt"
    57→    assert "Nike" in prompt, "Brand should be in prompt"
    58→    assert "$20-$150" in prompt, "Price range should be in prompt"
    59→    assert "Air Force 1" in prompt, "Recent purchase should be in prompt"
    60→    assert "Tiered" in prompt or "Recent Purchases" in prompt, "Tiered purchase section should be in prompt"
    61→    assert "bomber jacket" in prompt, "Past session should be in prompt"
    62→    assert "Items shown: 3" in prompt, "Session state should be in prompt"
    63→    print("PASSED - All user data injected correctly")
    64→    print(f"\n--- First 500 chars ---\n{prompt[:500]}...")
    65→
    66→
    67→async def test_search_clothing_tool():
    68→    """Test the search_clothing tool against live Serper API."""
    69→    print("\n=== TEST 2: search_clothing Tool (Live API) ===\n")
    70→
    71→    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY")
    72→    if not api_key:
    73→        print("SKIPPED - No SERPER_API_KEY set")
    74→        return
    75→
    76→    result = await execute_tool(
    77→        tool_name="search_clothing",
    78→        tool_input={"query": "mens black minimalist sneakers", "num_results": 3},
    79→        user_context={},
    80→    )
    81→
    82→    assert "results" in result, f"Expected 'results' key, got: {result.keys()}"
    83→    assert len(result["results"]) > 0, "Should return at least 1 result"
    84→    assert "frontend_payload" not in result, "search_clothing should NOT include frontend_payload (curation goes through present_items)"
    85→
    86→    print(f"Got {len(result['results'])} results:")
    87→    for item in result["results"][:3]:
    88→        print(f"  - {item['title']} | {item['price']} | {item['source']}")
    89→
    90→    # Check structure
    91→    first = result["results"][0]
    92→    required_fields = ["title", "source", "price", "image_url", "link", "product_id"]
    93→    for field in required_fields:
    94→        assert field in first, f"Missing field: {field}"
    95→
    96→    print("PASSED - search_clothing returns results to Claude only (no frontend_payload)")
    97→
    98→    # Test present_items tool
    99→    print("\n--- present_items Tool ---")
   100→    curated = result["results"][:2]  # Pick top 2
   101→    present_result = await execute_tool(
   102→        tool_name="present_items",
   103→        tool_input={"items": curated},
   104→        user_context={},
   105→    )
   106→    assert "frontend_payload" in present_result, "present_items SHOULD include frontend_payload"
   107→    assert present_result["frontend_payload"]["type"] == "clothing_results"
   108→    assert present_result["presented"] == 2
   109→    print(f"Presented {present_result['presented']} items via present_items")
   110→    print("PASSED - present_items creates frontend_payload for Socket.io broadcast")
   111→
   112→
   113→async def test_claude_with_mira():
   114→    """Test a real Claude API call with Mira's personality and tools."""
   115→    print("\n=== TEST 3: Live Claude Call as Mira ===\n")
   116→
   117→    auth_token = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN")
   118→    if not auth_token:
   119→        print("SKIPPED - No ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN set")
   120→        return
   121→
   122→    import anthropic
   123→
   124→    client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(
   125→        auth_token=auth_token,
   126→        default_headers={"anthropic-beta": "oauth-2025-04-20"},
   127→        default_query={"beta": "true"},
   128→    )
   129→
   130→    system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   131→        user_profile=MOCK_PROFILE,
   132→        purchases=MOCK_PURCHASES,
   133→        session_history=MOCK_PAST_SESSIONS,
   134→    )
   135→
   136→    # Test 1: Session opener
   137→    print("--- Mira's Opening Line ---")
   138→    response = await client.messages.create(
   139→        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   140→        max_tokens=200,
   141→        system=system_prompt,
   142→        messages=[
   143→            {"role": "user", "content": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself and start the session."},
   144→        ],
   145→        tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   146→    )
   147→
   148→    for block in response.content:
   149→        if hasattr(block, "text"):
   150→            print(f"Mira: {block.text}")
   151→        elif block.type == "tool_use":
   152→            print(f"[Tool call: {block.name}({json.dumps(block.input)})]")
   153→
   154→    print(f"\nTokens used: {response.usage.input_tokens} in / {response.usage.output_tokens} out")
   155→
   156→    # Test 2: Gesture response (thumbs down)
   157→    print("\n--- Mira Reacts to Thumbs Down ---")
   158→    response2 = await client.messages.create(
   159→        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   160→        max_tokens=200,
   161→        system=system_prompt,
   162→        messages=[
   163→            {"role": "user", "content": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself."},
   164→            {"role": "assistant", "content": response.content},
   165→            {"role": "user", "content": "The user gave a thumbs down (dislike this item)."},
   166→        ],
   167→        tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   168→    )
   169→
   170→    for block in response2.content:
   171→        if hasattr(block, "text"):
   172→            print(f"Mira: {block.text}")
   173→        elif block.type == "tool_use":
   174→            print(f"[Tool call: {block.name}({json.dumps(block.input)})]")
   175→
   176→    # Test 3: Voice input
   177→    print("\n--- Mira Responds to Voice ---")
   178→    response3 = await client.messages.create(
   179→        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   180→        max_tokens=200,
   181→        system=system_prompt,
   182→        messages=[
   183→            {"role": "user", "content": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself."},
   184→            {"role": "assistant", "content": response.content},
   185→            {"role": "user", "content": "Show me some cool jackets for spring"},
   186→        ],
   187→        tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   188→    )
   189→
   190→    for block in response3.content:
   191→        if hasattr(block, "text"):
   192→            print(f"Mira: {block.text}")
   193→        elif block.type == "tool_use":
   194→            print(f"[Tool call: {block.name}({json.dumps(block.input)})]")
   195→
   196→    print("\nPASSED - Mira responds in character with tool use")
   197→
   198→
   199→async def main():
   200→    print("=" * 60)
   201→    print("  MIRA AGENT INTEGRATION TESTS")
   202→    print("=" * 60)
   203→
   204→    # Test 1: Pure Python, no API needed
   205→    test_system_prompt()
   206→
   207→    # Test 2: Needs SERPER_API_KEY
   208→    await test_search_clothing_tool()
   209→
   210→    # Test 3: Needs ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
   211→    await test_claude_with_mira()
   212→
   213→    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
   214→    print("  ALL TESTS COMPLETE")
   215→    print("=" * 60)
   216→
   217→
   218→if __name__ == "__main__":
   219→    asyncio.run(main())
   220→
